element: Radioactive: Tc, Po, At, Ra, Rn, actinides (U, Th...)

content: Toxic/poisonous - eliminated; confirmed by EU 10/2011 'ND', REACH Annex XVII, RoHS.
element: Toxic: As, Cd, Pb, Hg, Be, Se, Tl
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Toxic: As, Cd, Pb, Hg, Be, Se, Tl (Safety)

id: chunk|rule|Reactive/burning: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs (Mg, Ca limited)|safety-all
content: Highly reactive/flammable; Mg/Ca too reactive for clean alloying/handling.
element: Reactive/burning: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs (Mg, Ca limited)
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Reactive/burning: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs (Mg, Ca limited) (Safety)

content: Extensive solid solubility in fcc Au at high T (Au3Mn, AuMn order). Best-soluble TM marker. MA ~1 wt%.
Appreciable terminal fcc solubility (several at%) before AuIn/AuIn2. Tested by PM. MA ~1 wt%.
Limited but nonzero Au(fcc) solubility; rich intermetallic field; low MP eases MA. Tested by PM.
Modest terminal solubility; forms Au4Ti etc. Detectable to 12 ppm. Low at% marker.
Limited Au(fcc) solubility; Au-rich intermetallics. Detectable ~30 ppm. Low-loading only.
Restricted solubility; intermetallics dominate (Zr-like).
Limited solubility (<~4 wt%); detectable to 3 ppm. Good dilute marker.
Terminal fcc solubility at high T (~<2 wt%) but ordered/decomposes on cooling. Reg. limit issue (3-10 ppm).
Eutectic with large solid-state miscibility gap; small low-T solubility. Detectable 12 ppm.
Au-Sc intermetallics (e.g. AuSc); low terminal solubility. Marker as dilute/compound.

id: chunk|rule|Y, La, Ce, Pr, Nd (light RE)|gold-solubility-gold
content: Au-RE line compounds (strong negative dH); negligible terminal solid solubility (~0). Marker sits as 2nd phase.
element: Y, La, Ce, Pr, Nd (light RE)
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Y, La, Ce, Pr, Nd (light RE) (Gold solubility)

id: chunk|rule|Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, Yb, Lu (heavy RE)|gold-solubility-gold
content: Expected line-compound formers, negligible Au solubility (by analogy; no individual phase-diagram papers).
element: Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, Yb, Lu (heavy RE)
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, Yb, Lu (heavy RE) (Gold solubility)

content: Some terminal solubility then AuSn/AuSn2/AuSn4 (key solder). Watch low-MP eutectic.
Au-Al intermetallics ('purple plague' AuAl2); limited Au-side solubility; reactive. Alloyed to 20% in jewelry.
Low solubility; Nb3Au (A15) compounds. Marginal as dilute marker; high MP.
Simple eutectic ~361C; near-zero solid solubility (<~1 at%). Classic Au-Ge die-attach solder; marker as 2nd phase.
Au-Sr intermetallics; negligible solubility; Sr reactivity/volatility complicates MA.
Very limited solubility + refractory MP 3017C makes MA impractical; Ta3Au-type compounds.
Au-Mo essentially immiscible (large miscibility gaps), no compounds; MP 2623C. Will not alloy uniformly.
Au-W essentially immiscible, no phases, near-zero solubility; MP 3422C.
Au-Re near-complete immiscibility; MP 3186C.
Eutectic + AuSb2; Sb solid solubility <~1 at%. Marker as compound, not solution. Reg. limit ~10 ppm.
Eutectic + AuTe2 (calaverite); negligible solubility. Brittle at >0.1 wt%.
Eutectic + Au2Bi; very low Bi solubility. Spec limit ~20 ppm in PM gold.
Classic simple eutectic (~363C, 18.6 at%); mutual solid solubility ~nil. Marker as 2nd phase.
Complete/near-complete fcc solid solution - the 'fully soluble' benchmarks. Already present in PM gold -> not used as markers.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Cu, Ag, Pd, Pt (reference cases) (Gold solubility)

content: Zr neodecanoate/octoate commercial & oil-soluble; ~0 native background; no P/S. BENCHMARK marker. Ash-forming (tiny at ppm).
Ti neodecanoate is an established AW/FM additive -> finished oils may ALREADY contain Ti (background). Verify base oil.

id: chunk|rule|Y, La, Ce, Nd, Yb + RE|oil-compatibility-engine-oil
content: RE neodecanoates/2-EH available; RE absent from oil additives & wear metals -> clean, distinctive. No P/S. Ce/Nd also fuel-catalytic.
element: Y, La, Ce, Nd, Yb + RE
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Y, La, Ce, Nd, Yb + RE (Oil compatibility)

content: Mn 2-EH/naphthenate oil-soluble; trace wear background. Caution: Mn(acac) low decomp; MMT->Mn3O4 catalyst fouling if in fuel.
Co 2-EH/naphthenate classic driers, oil-soluble; not an oil additive (clean). Caution: redox/pro-oxidant - keep 5-15 ppm.
Vanadyl acac/2-EH oil-soluble but VO(acac)2 decomposes ~400C (engine hot-zone); V background from HFO; catalyst-active; ash.
Hf mirrors Zr but commercial oil-soluble Hf scarce (custom synth); not in standard ICP panels (method extension).
No simple oil-soluble W carboxylate; delivered as WS2 (adds S) or W-carbonyl. Clean background if a soluble form secured.
Mainstream FM (MoDTC) present 50-250 ppm in many oils & in ICP panels -> marker dose buried; reactive with ZDDP/detergents.
Carboxylate/beta-diketonate ligands are C/H/O only -> add NO P or S; preserve ZDDP P-window (GF-6 0.06-0.08%) & S caps. Major advantage.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — All RE + Zr/Ti/Hf carboxylates & diketonates (Oil compatibility)

content: Every metal marker forms sulfated ash (D874). At 5-50 ppm increment is <<0.01% - negligible vs ACEA C (0.5-0.8%) & ILSAC GF-7/API SQ (0.9%), but tally in low-SAPS oils near the ceiling.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — All metal markers (ash) (Oil compatibility)

content: High volatility (sublime <100C) risks marker loss via evaporation (Noack)/maldistribution. Prefer low-volatility neodecanoate/naphthenate.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Volatile diketonates (TMHD/acac of Fe,Mn,Ni,V; Y-TMHD) (Oil compatibility)

content: API 1509 explicitly permits markers that 'distinguish product without materially impacting performance'; conformance audits compare measured vs filed chemistry.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Declared performance-neutral marker (Oil compatibility)

content: Sesquioxides/CeO2 thermally stable far above melt temps; survive compounding. Disperse: control particle size / silane.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Rare-earth oxides (CeO2, La2O3, Nd2O3...) (Process / thermal)

content: Acid-insoluble & thermally stable; pigment-grade RE oxides resist solvents/chemicals.
RE3+ coordinates cellulose -OH; improves dye fixation; wash-durable to 50 cycles. Used as covert ICP-OES marker.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — RE salt (applied bath) (Process / thermal)

content: Weather-, light-, chemical-stable; high NIR reflectance; non-toxic.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — RE-oxide / RE-Mo-oxide pigment (Process / thermal)

content: Coated rutile suppresses photocatalytic radical generation that chalks the binder.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — TiO2 rutile, surface-coated (SiO2/Al2O3/ZrO2) (Process / thermal)

content: Photocatalytically degrades the organic matrix under UV. Indoor-only, or pick another element.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — TiO2 anatase or uncoated (Process / thermal)

content: Survives PC/epoxy processing; acid-resistant; raises thermal stability at low loadings.
Raises composite thermal stability; tolerated to high loadings (transition ~730C >> processing).
Fine oxide powders disperse & are processing-stable (Sb2O3 fire-reactive with halogens = functional).
Robust to high-T processing.
Comparatively volatile / sublimes & reduces (MoO3->Mo4O11) at elevated T - marker loss risk in hot/reducing melts. Prefer WO3/tungstate.
Nitrates decompose ~190-300C (-> oxide + NO2); gassing/voids/colour. Convert to oxide before compounding.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Metal nitrate (e.g. Ce(NO3)3) (Process / thermal)

content: Stable through normal melt temps (SrCO3 decomp ~875C). Survive processing (not pure oxide, but metal-calibrated).
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Metal carbonate (SrCO3, RE carbonates) (Process / thermal)

content: Decompose low (hydroxides <150-340C; acetates ~110-250C) releasing volatiles/water -> foaming. Prefer oxide.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Metal acetate / hydroxide (Process / thermal)

content: OXIDES = universally robust (thermal+chemical). Nitrate/acetate/hydroxide OK for water-based textile/coating but risky in polymer melt.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Marker-form general rule (Process / thermal)

content: Inferred Go (acid/oxidiser-insoluble) but NO direct study of marker survival through H2O2/hydrosulfite bleach - key experimental gap.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — RE/Ti/Zr oxide vs bleaching (Process / thermal)

content: EU 10/2011: SUM of all lanthanides <= 0.05 mg/kg. Multiple RE markers share one tiny migration budget. Choose low-migration carriers.
Co compounds = REACH SVHC (CMR 1B) + skin sensitizer; food SML 0.05; OEKO-TEX 1.0 (Class I). Riskiest single marker.
Food SML 0.04 mg/kg (low); OEKO-TEX extractable ~30 mg/kg; Sb2O3 suspected carcinogen.
EU 10/2011 SMLs: Al 1.0, Ba 1.0, Mn 0.6 mg/kg.
NIOSH/OSHA OEL ~0.1 mg/m3 (low) - occupational watch-out even though finished articles lightly regulated.
Cr(VI) restricted (REACH XVII 47; leather 0.0003%). Keep marker as Cr metal / Cr(III).
REACH Annex XVII Entry 27: Ni release 0.5 ug/cm2/wk (0.2 piercing). Sensitizer benchmark for skin-contact codes.
sourceTitle: Compatibility Rules — Nickel (benchmark; not in palette) (Regulatory)

content: TiO2 color additive (US 21 CFR 73.575, Ti <=1% of food). EU delisted E171 as food ADDITIVE (2022) but polymer-pigment use differs - confirm per use.
SiO2 GRAS (21 CFR 182.90). Benign.
